name: finance_tips
tone_preamble: You are a sharp, friendly personal finance advisor sharing wealth-building secrets and money hacks for TikTok and YouTube Shorts. Your tone is energetic, direct, and actionable. Avoid complex jargon—make money simple and exciting.
subscribe_cta: Subscribe for daily money secrets to build your wealth!

finance_hacks.title: The 8th Wonder of the World
finance_hacks.tip: Starting to invest just 5 years earlier can literally double your retirement net worth because of compounding growth.
finance_hacks.example: If you invest $200 a month starting at age 20, you'll have over $1.2 million by age 65. Start at age 25, and you will only have around $700,000.
finance_hacks.action_step: Open a low-cost index fund account today and set up automatic monthly contributions.

finance_hacks.title: Stop Leaving Money on the Table
finance_hacks.tip: Traditional banks pay a microscopic 0.01% interest, while High-Yield Savings Accounts pay up to 4% or 5% interest.
finance_hacks.example: $10,000 in a traditional bank earns just $1 a year. In a High-Yield Savings Account, that same $10,000 earns $400 to $500 a year, completely risk-free.
finance_hacks.action_step: Search for top-rated HYSA online and move your emergency fund there immediately.

finance_hacks.title: Budget Without Being Miserable
finance_hacks.tip: Split your income into three buckets: 50% for Needs, 30% for Wants, and 20% for Savings and investing.
finance_hacks.example: If you make $3,000 a month, spend $1,500 on rent and bills, keep $900 for eating out and fun, and auto-transfer $600 to investments.
finance_hacks.action_step: Track your last month's spending and categorize it into these three buckets to see where you stand.

finance_hacks.title: Conquer Your Debt Faster!
finance_hacks.tip: Pay off your smallest debt first to build momentum, then roll that payment into the next smallest debt.
finance_hacks.example: Imagine you have a $500 credit card debt and a $1500 personal loan. Pay off the credit card first, then take the money you were paying on it and add it to your personal loan payment.
finance_hacks.action_step: List all your debts from smallest to largest balance and commit to tackling the first one aggressively.

finance_hacks.title: Set It and Forget It!
finance_hacks.tip: Make saving automatic by setting up recurring transfers from your checking to your savings account right after payday.
finance_hacks.example: If you get paid bi-weekly, set up a $50 transfer to your savings every two weeks. You won't miss it, and you'll save $1300 a year!
finance_hacks.action_step: Log into your bank's online portal and set up an automatic transfer to your savings for your next payday.

finance_hacks.title: Your Credit Score Supercharger
finance_hacks.tip: Pay your bills on time, keep credit utilization low (below 30%), and don't close old credit accounts.
finance_hacks.example: If your credit limit is $1000, try to keep your balance under $300. This signals responsible credit use and boosts your score.
finance_hacks.action_step: Check your credit report for free at AnnualCreditReport.com and identify one area to improve this month.

finance_hacks.title: Unsubscribe to Savings!
finance_hacks.tip: Review all your recurring subscriptions and cancel any you don't actively use or truly value.
finance_hacks.example: You might be paying for a streaming service you rarely watch or a gym membership you don't use. Canceling just three $10/month services saves you $360 a year.
finance_hacks.action_step: Check your bank statements for the last three months and list all recurring charges. Cancel one today!

finance_hacks.title: Get Paid to Spend!
finance_hacks.tip: Use cashback credit cards, browser extensions, or shopping apps to earn money back on your everyday purchases.
finance_hacks.example: A 2% cashback card on $1500 in monthly spending earns you $30 back each month, or $360 per year, just for buying what you already need.
finance_hacks.action_step: Research cashback credit cards or download a reputable cashback app like Rakuten or Ibotta before your next shopping trip.

finance_hacks.title: Your Financial Safety Net
finance_hacks.tip: Build an emergency fund with 3-6 months of living expenses to cover unexpected costs like job loss or medical emergencies.
finance_hacks.example: If your monthly expenses are $2000, aim for $6000-$12,000 saved. This prevents debt when life throws a curveball.
finance_hacks.action_step: Decide on a small, achievable amount (e.g., $25) to transfer to a dedicated savings account for your emergency fund this week.

finance_hacks.title: Don't Pay Full Price!
finance_hacks.tip: Call your service providers (internet, cable, insurance) annually to ask for discounts, better rates, or to price match competitors.
finance_hacks.example: Many internet providers will offer a loyalty discount if you just ask, or match a competitor's lower rate, saving you $10-20 a month.
finance_hacks.action_step: Pick one bill (e.g., internet or phone) and call their customer service today to inquire about lower rates or special offers.

finance_hacks.title: Free Money for Retirement!
finance_hacks.tip: If your employer offers a 401(k) or 403(b) match, contribute at least enough to get the full match – it's literally free money.
finance_hacks.example: If your company matches 50% of your contributions up to 6% of your salary, contributing $3000 gets you an extra $1500 for retirement.
finance_hacks.action_step: Check with your HR department or plan administrator to find out your company's matching policy and adjust your contributions if you're not getting the full match.

finance_hacks.title: Cook Once, Eat Cheap All Week
finance_hacks.tip: Plan and prepare your meals in advance for the week to avoid expensive impulse buys, takeout, and food waste.
finance_hacks.example: Spending 2-3 hours on Sunday to prep lunches and dinners for the week can cut your food budget by $50-$100, saving you over $2000 a year.
finance_hacks.action_step: Choose one meal (e.g., lunch) to meal prep for next week and buy ingredients specifically for it.

finance_hacks.title: Slash Your Student Loan Payments
finance_hacks.tip: If you have good credit, consider refinancing private student loans to a lower interest rate, potentially saving thousands over the loan's life.
finance_hacks.example: Refinancing a $30,000 loan from 6.8% to 4.5% could save you over $3,000 in interest and lower your monthly payment by $25-$50.
finance_hacks.action_step: Research reputable student loan refinancing lenders and use an online calculator to see your potential savings.

finance_hacks.title: Every Penny Counts
finance_hacks.tip: Collect all your loose change, small cash windfalls, or even $1 bills you find and put them into a dedicated savings jar.
finance_hacks.example: If you put just $2 a day into a jar from loose change or skipping a small indulgence, you'll save over $700 in a year without feeling a pinch.
finance_hacks.action_step: Find a jar or container and commit to dropping all your loose change or small unexpected cash into it daily.

finance_hacks.title: Never Pay More Than You Have To
finance_hacks.tip: Before making a significant purchase, quickly check competitors' prices or ask retailers if they offer price matching.
finance_hacks.example: Many big box stores will match an online competitor's price, saving you the hassle of driving around and ensuring you get the best deal.
finance_hacks.action_step: Before your next planned purchase over $50, spend 5 minutes checking at least two other retailers or online stores for a better price.

finance_hacks.title: Your Budget Reset Button
finance_hacks.tip: Challenge yourself to a period (e.g., a weekend, a week) where you only spend money on absolute necessities.
finance_hacks.example: A 'no-spend' weekend can save you $50-$200 that you might have otherwise spent on dining out, entertainment, or impulse shopping, helping you reset your spending habits.
finance_hacks.action_step: Plan a no-spend day this coming weekend. Prepare your meals, find free entertainment, and avoid non-essential purchases.

finance_hacks.title: Simple Path to Wealth
finance_hacks.tip: Instead of picking individual stocks, invest in broad market index funds (like an S&P 500 fund) for diversification and lower fees.
finance_hacks.example: Over the long term, index funds have historically matched market returns (around 7-10% annually), often outperforming actively managed funds and significantly building wealth.
finance_hacks.action_step: Research a low-cost S&P 500 index fund or ETF (e.g., VOO, SPY) and consider opening an investment account to get started.

finance_hacks.title: Crush High-Interest Debt First!
finance_hacks.tip: Focus on paying off debts with the highest interest rates first, saving you the most money on interest over time.
finance_hacks.example: If you have a credit card at 20% APR and a personal loan at 10% APR, paying extra on the credit card first will reduce your overall interest payments significantly faster.
finance_hacks.action_step: List all your debts by interest rate (highest to lowest) and direct any extra payments towards the debt at the top of your list.

saving_tips.description: Before buying anything non-essential over $50, wait exactly 48 hours. If you still want it, buy it. 80% of the time, the urge will completely pass.
saving_tips.benefit: Saves hundreds of dollars monthly on impulse purchases you didn't actually need.

saving_tips.description: Review your bank statement and cancel subscriptions you haven't used in the last 30 days. Streaming services, apps, and memberships add up fast.
saving_tips.benefit: Instantly puts $50 to $100 back into your pocket every single month.

templates.name: wealth_hack
templates.system_prompt: Create an exciting wealth building hack. Start with a hook about how most people are losing money, present the concept, give a concrete mathematical example, and end with the action step.
templates.title_template: How to Hack Compound Interest to Get Rich! 💸🚀

templates.name: saving_tip
templates.system_prompt: Deliver a punchy money saving tip. Start with a hook about saving money effortlessly, describe the rule/method, and explain the financial benefit.
templates.title_template: This Simple Hack Will Save You THOUSANDS! 💰😲
